figures.caption: Figure 1. Source-family routing ablations: baseline (recency-biaswrapped vanilla Qwen3) vs. Block AttnRes trained from scratch, on the same probe and the same data. Three families produce strong AttnRes-side effects with negligible baseline counterparts; a fourth carries routing mass but no causal weight.

Figure 2. Sublayer-restricted routing ablations on the AttnRes checkpoint. embedding matters only through MLP; nonlocal only through attention; current through both, with attention larger; prev completed through neither.
Figure 3. Layer-binned sublayer ablations on the AttnRes checkpoint as a heatmap. A small number of localised cells carry the primary causal signal.

Figure 4. Routing-mass composition by sublayer. The baseline allocates ≈ 0.84 to current in both sublayers; the AttnRes attention sublayer is broadly distributed with the largest slice on nonlocal, while the AttnRes MLP sublayer is current-heavy with non-trivial embedding and prev completed slices.
